qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated public-chatbotConfig endpoint discloses private chatflow flowData
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets unauthenticated disclosure in the public-chatbotConfig chatflows controller/service path, while the advisory targets unsanitized user input passed into GraphCypherQAChain.invoke in packages/components/nodes/chains/GraphCypherQAChain/GraphCypherQAChain.ts and executed as Cypher against Neo4j.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described GraphCypherQAChain Cypher-injection root cause. The only qualifying finding is an unrelated unauthenticated disclosure in the public-chatbotConfig endpoint.
evidence: The advisory JSON describes Cypher injection in packages/components/nodes/chains/GraphCypherQAChain/GraphCypherQAChain.ts lines 193-219, where user input is passed directly into chain.invoke and executed on a Neo4j-backed GraphCypherQAChain chatflow.
The run manifest lists exactly one final qualifying finding: /Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-DryRun/advisory_d7ffcc3d/private_chatflow_config_disclosure.

That finding's vulnerability report identifies GET /api/v1/public-chatbotConfig/:id and code locations in repo/packages/server/src/controllers/chatflows/index.ts:240, repo/packages/server/src/services/chatflows/index.ts:382, and repo/packages/server/src/utils/getUploadsConfig.ts:20, which are different components and a different root cause from GraphCypherQAChain input-to-Neo4j execution.

The finding's PoC report reproduces anonymous leakage of TOPSECRET_PROMPT from private chatflow flowData; it does not exercise GraphCypherQAChain, Neo4j, Cypher execution, or the advisory's vulnerable source-to-sink path.
